title: Stelis cordilabia
taxonomy: Stelis cordilabia is a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Stelis and the family Orchidaceae. The Stelis genus is a group of small to medium-sized orchids, often found in tropical regions. The family Orchidaceae is the largest family of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species. Stelis cordilabia is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, a large and diverse group of orchids.

intro: Stelis cordilabia is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its slender stems and small, white flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis cordilabia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stelis cordilabia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
